unmapped.human_action: Review photonic computing watchlist (LITE, COHR) for options-chain viability: pull current chains to establish thesis-implied vs. market-implied move ratio before sizing. Confirm scanner configuration includes LITE and COHR as monitored tickers. Determine whether scanner absence reflects thin liquidity on existing chains or systematic exclusion from scan parameters — these have different implications for the trade. If LITE June or September 2026 OTM calls show implied volatility below 40% while OCS backlog conversion remains on schedule, the entry condition is met.
unmapped.legal_disclaimer: Internal editorial candidate for operator review only. Not investment advice. All book positions referenced are paper-trading entries in a research context. Past implied-ratio gaps do not predict future option pricing relationships.
unmapped.machine_action: Monitor LITE and COHR quarterly disclosures for OCS backlog conversion rate, NVIDIA purchase-commitment burn cadence, and hyperscaler optical revenue line-item disclosure. Flag any scanner pass for LITE, COHR, or AAOI immediately. Track Ayar Labs and Lightmatter for additional financing or hyperscaler design-win disclosures. Monitor MRVL earnings for Celestial AI revenue contribution vs. the $500M ARR Q4 FY2028 guide. Re-run scanner against LITE/COHR chains weekly to detect first moment of options-market discovery.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Corpus confidence scores from trend-corpus claims as of 2026-05-17. Scanner results from scan-results.json dated 2026-04-28 UTC. Book positions from paper-journal/mispricing as of 2026-05-29. Cross-sector private-mark-to-options-pricing lag is observational pattern analysis, not back-tested statistical regularity.
unmapped.position_conflict_disclosure: Book holds open CRSP call spreads (entered at 2.1-2.2x thesis-implied vs. market-implied) referenced in this issue as a cross-sector analogy; those positions are paper-trade entries, not live book. Book also holds open cicadas/ENSO positions in FXA, CORN, DBA, WEAT expiring June 2026 — unrelated to the photonic computing thesis. No current exposure to LITE, COHR, AAOI, Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, or Marvell in either paper or live book.
unmapped.uncertainty: Scanner absence could reflect genuinely thin options liquidity on LITE and COHR rather than options-market underpricing of the thesis. If chains are too thin to establish a position at acceptable bid-ask cost, the quiet setup cannot be expressed through options and the equity-long risk profile is substantially different. The cross-sector lag hypothesis has one validated observation period (CRSP entered at 2.1-2.2x implied ratio) but that book position expired at zero P&L — the corpus-implied move occurred without the options move, which is either timing uncertainty or a boundary on how far the mispricing extends. Sizing should reflect this.

original_issue.falsification: Primary: NVIDIA restructures or reduces the $4B LITE/COHR commitment in any 8-K, 10-Q, or earnings-call disclosure — this removes the customer anchor the revaluation rests on and breaks the thesis. Secondary: LITE or COHR misses hyperscaler optical revenue or OCS backlog conversion by more than 25% in one quarter, breaking the revenue-inflection case. Tertiary: a non-photonic interconnect alternative wins material design-in slots away from LITE/COHR/Lightmatter/Ayar at OFC 2026-2027 equivalent events. Cross-sector validity check: if CRSP Casgevy patient-initiation count declines for two consecutive quarters, the foundational claim that corpus-high/scanner-dark sectors have durable asymmetry loses its primary validated instance.

original_issue.thesis: Photonic computing has cleared three simultaneous conditions for a quiet setup: NVIDIA-anchored revenue inflection at 0.88 corpus confidence, private chiplet validation at multi-billion-dollar marks, and zero passing options contracts on LITE or COHR. The scanner's loudest signals sit in peak-hype names where the narrative has fully taxed the trade. The cross-sector pattern — private marks leading public options pricing by 12-24 months — is documented consistently across synthetic biology, defense AI, longevity, and edge AI. The asymmetric position today is in the sectors where corpus confidence is highest and scanner coverage is absent.
original_issue.uncertainty: Scanner absence could reflect genuinely thin options liquidity on LITE and COHR rather than options-market underpricing of the thesis. If chains are too thin to establish a position at acceptable bid-ask cost, the quiet setup cannot be expressed through options and the equity-long risk profile is substantially different. The cross-sector lag hypothesis has one validated observation period (CRSP entered at 2.1-2.2x implied ratio) but that book position expired at zero P&L — the corpus-implied move occurred without the options move, which is either timing uncertainty or a boundary on how far the mispricing extends. Sizing should reflect this.
